text:
BOTTOM LINE — Risk-off, cyclicals and duration plays dumped. SPY fell 1.25% with breadth weak and equal-weight RSP down 1.50%, while factor leadership stayed with VLUE and MTUM despite de-risking into the Fed with VIX up to 18.4 and VIXY gaining 4.03%. STYLE showed SMALL > LARGE by 0.26pp, but the leadership came from defensives-in-name-only like XBI and DRAM while high-duration growth and precious metals were sold hard alongside a stronger dollar and higher Nasdaq vol.

INDICES — VIX closed at 18.4, above its 20-day 17.4. SOXX +1.44% led, then IWM -0.75%, DIA -0.99%, QQQ -1.01%, and SPY -1.25%, with RSP -1.50% the weakest and STYLE SMALL > LARGE by 0.26pp. The standout cross-asset move was SLV -4.39% with GLD -2.27% against UUP +0.90% and modest rates, signaling a sharp real-asset de-rating.

VOLUME — SPY volume ran 1.9x and QQQ volume was implicitly elevated via VXN 28.6 versus its 20-day 25.7, both with prices down, which is classic equity distribution. XLRE at 2.4x, XLC at 1.6x, and XLP at 1.5x on heavy selling versus flat XLK volume at 1.0x show money rotating out of bond-proxy and communication sectors into higher-beta factor baskets like XBI at 2.1x, IPAY at 2.8x, and PAVE at 2.8x.

SECTORS & ROTATION — XLI Industrials -0.14%, XLK Technology -0.34%, XLF Financials -0.55%, XLE Energy -1.25%, XLU Utilities -1.33%, XLB Materials -1.33%, XLV Healthcare -1.46%, XLP Staples -2.23%, XLY Discretionary -2.51%, XLRE Real Estate -2.51%, XLC Comms -2.78%. Top industries were XBI +2.99% and DRAM +2.69%, while XTN -3.36% and TAN -3.52% lagged with SLV -4.39% and SILJ -3.70%. The tape showed money-in to biotech and DRAM within semis while money-out hit transports, solar, and precious-metals miners despite SOXX +1.44%. This is a narrow factor-and-theme tape with defensives, bond proxies, and metals sold, so label it as AI hardware leading.

POSITIONING — SPY PCR_vol at 0.57 with ATM_IV 14.3% and positive skew 7.4% signals call-heavy complacency layered over put-demand hedging in the tails. QQQ PCR_vol at 1.17 and IWM PCR_vol at 1.81 with skews of 12.2% and 8.3% show concentrated protection demand in growth and small caps as VXN 28.6 and RVX 24.9 both sit above their 20-day averages, while VIX remains only modestly elevated.

CROSS-ASSET — Dollar strength with UUP +0.90% alongside GLD -2.27% and SLV -4.39% is the key transmission, tightening financial conditions for real assets while risk-assets in equities only partially de-risked. Oil softness with USO -1.07% plus XLE -1.25% underscores macro disinflation pressure feeding directly into the Energy complex.

SIGNALS & WATCH — Fear & Greed at 54.4 sits neutral, with XBI +2.99% (biotech), DRAM +2.69% (memory), and SOXX +1.44% (semis) the top momentum leaders, while XLC -2.78% is the worst sector and SLV -4.39% the worst basket on 20-day terms. KLAC printed 4.3x volume and +0.59% in The Fab 7, signaling accumulation under AI hardware leadership, while IPAY at 2.8x and -2.03% showed payment-tech distribution on heavy selling. EWP 5.9x with flat price and XLRE 2.4x with -2.51% highlight country and REIT stress without clear macro catalysts beyond the stronger dollar and higher real yields. Watch GLD -2.27% for a potential gamma flip toward its 370.0 GEX level, SLV -4.39% with ATM_IV 49.6% and PCR_vol 1.36 for vol-crush or follow-through, and XLC -2.78% with 1.6x volume as potential source of funds if Fed rhetoric stays hawkish.
